item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think it's an entirely rogue group that's just familiar with the Hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love Hand articles and all (and this sort of snarky activism is exactly how I picture some members operating), but I think it could benefit from a clarification that this is the work of one/two people and not the entirety of the Serpent's Hand. I'm not sure which is funnier, the Talk to the Hand line or the idea that this could be the master plan concocted by fifty-or-so magicians.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Originally read just "Special-Purpose Concrete" and wondered what the SPC was up to this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A relatively uninteresting premise but that "Talk to the Hand" bit was just too golden to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels like it falls flat, like, it's a decent concept, but I think more could have been done with it, and as it is now, it doesn't feel satisfying to read (which, I know is the point of a shaggy dog story, but this one doesn't have the build up that I think would justify the ending)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UNSUB: Yeah, yeah. Talk to the Hand.

Honestly I read this line and I just about died laughing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is damn cool :3 I guess I'm not a huge fan of the shaggy dog ending but like, I think the rest of the article is easily strong enough to compensate for it.

+ minor things
- minor things

This would occur even without being connected to an electrical current.

I'd probably make it explicit that you mean the cooler here; it feels kinda awkward as a sentence without it.

transporting subjects who entered to an unknown

I feel like there should be an "it" after "entered"; it's not strictly necessary but it flows better with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah we don’t need to jump down a persons throat for just trying to help guys. Especially since they weren’t totally off-base.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had it classified as Euclid because in my mind that’s the default object classification of new anomalies and this was Neutralized before it could be given further classification. I think that if this had been in containment long-term it probably would’ve gotten a Safe classification so you’re on the money there. You’re also right about danger not being the primary determinant of classification and I always appreciate that being brought up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, just because someone's a veteran doesn't make their work untouchable. Seems like the criticism was offered in good faith.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a slight difference between wanting to help and copypasting the entire Object Class locked box test, especially when the majority of it doesn't apply to this SCP. At least only put the sections which are relevant.

Also, I do not agree with your assessment.

Assessed as a potential source of inter-dimensional portals

If there are interdimensional portals, it can be assumed that there is a small chance of object emerging which could require more substantial containment than just a box. Therefore, it would not be able to be left alone, as we are not entirely sure what would happen. For this reason, I support the Euclid classification.

Edit: I was not trying to jump down this person's throat or anything like that. I just noticed that an object class discussion was going on and thought I'd give my view.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt dirty upvoting another infinite space article. I'm going to say now that this will be the last infinite space I ever upvote. until the next one is posted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no. I am just trying to help

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you… trying to correct a highly experienced author who also happens to be one of the site admins?

EDIT: Just to be clear, the rationale behind a Euclid classification is that, in this case, there's no way to simply hide a public building.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Class should have been "Neutralized (Formerly Safe)"

What is the Locked Box Test?
The Locked Box Test is an informal guideline used to determine an object's most appropriate Object Class. It goes like this:

If you lock it in a box, leave it alone, and nothing bad will happen, then it's probably Safe.
If you lock it in a box, leave it alone, and you're not entirely sure what will happen, then it's probably Euclid.
If you lock it in a box, leave it alone, and it easily escapes, then it's probably Keter.
If it is the box, then it's probably Thaumiel.

If an SCP is very dangerous should its Object Class be higher?

No, danger does not really affect an SCP's Object Class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had this idea for the shaggy dog portion of JamCon and it stuck with me ever since. Thanks to Vezaz, Balthazar, Mewitverse, NamelessMediocre, Dr Spookimoto, Taylor, and rattles for crit. Still looking for a title.

Image is photo taken by me on my phone at work.
